name: idd-route
description: v0.2.1: generation-neutral candidate naming (#251) — codex candidate renamed codex-xhigh (model generation now pinned solely in issue-driven-dev's codex-call default); old jsonl records keep the historical name (append-only), new records use codex-xhigh, old bucket decays out on the 30-day half-life. v0.2.0: Pin to idd-route-swift v0.3.0 — adds update-outcome (idd-close calls this to finalize merged/abandoned) and backfill (one-shot seed from past GitHub verify-comment history; uses gh CLI). The /idd-route:backfill skill now actually works (was placeholder in v0.1.0). idd-close Step 4.5 in issue-driven-dev v2.38.0+ now succeeds instead of gracefully no-op'ing on command-not-found. v0.1.0: Data-driven agent routing for IDD methodology. Recommends Codex (GPT-5.5 xhigh) vs Claude (Opus 4.7 / Sonnet 4.6 / Haiku 4.5) per IDD issue based on observed track record per (complexity, scope_class) bucket with exponential decay (recent decisions weight more, half-life 30 days). Falls back to static heuristic rubric when fewer than 5 data points exist for a candidate (cold start). Wraps the PsychQuant/idd-route-swift binary (universal arm64+x86_64), auto-downloaded via bin/idd-route-wrapper.sh on first use following the version-aware pattern from che-word-mcp. Three thin user-facing skills: /idd-route:recommend (ad-hoc lookup; the issue-driven-dev plugin's idd-diagnose calls the binary directly), /idd-route:stats (human-readable markdown summary by bucket), /idd-route:backfill (one-shot seed from past GH verify-comment history). Per-repo stats at <repo>/.claude/.idd/routing-stats.jsonl, opt-in global mirror at ~/.cache/idd-route/stats.jsonl for cross-repo learning. Opt-out via {enabled: false} in <repo>/.claude/idd-route.json. References controlled signal vocabulary (explicit_acceptance / single_handler / sibling_sweep_needed / etc) shipped in references/signal-vocabulary.md. Static heuristic rubric in references/static-heuristic.md.
